content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814
Technical Assistance/Administration . . . Executive/Legal . . . Enforcement/Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5660 322-5901
322-6441

March 8, 1976

Mr. John S. Gibson, Jr.
Councilman, 15th District
Room 375, Los Angeles City Hall
Los Angeles, Ca. 90012

Re: Request for Opinion
    No. 76-026

Dear Mr. Gibson:

     Thank you for your letter of February 19, 1976, requesting an
opinion regarding the appropriate code reviewing body for the Housing
Authority and Community Development Agency of the City of Los Angeles.
Because your letter raises no substantial question involving an inter-
pretation of the Political Reform Act, no formal opinion will be issued.
However, I hope the following informal comments will be helpful.

     Although a Housing Authority or Community Redevelopment Agency
may be a state agency for some purposes, Winkelman v. City of Pittsburg,
32 Cal.App. 3d 834, 846 (1973), for purposes of the Political Reform
Act they are not state agencies. See FPPC Regulation 2 Cal. Adm. Code
Section 18249 defining state agency. These agencies are city agencies
because they operate exclusively within the City of Los Angeles. More-
over, their members are appointed by the mayor, subject to confirmation
by the city council. Accordingly, the city council is the code review-
ing body for the Conflicts of Interest Codes of both agencies.

     If you have any questions, please contact Ken Goshorn, an attorney
in the Conflict of Interest Division.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Daniel H. Lowenstein
                                        Chairman,
                                        For the Commission

DHL:pvp

I can see this is a mostly blank white page with three small circular elements on the right side that appear to be loading spinners or decorative elements. There is no readable text content to transcribe from this image.
